mainHeading: RCMC Registration Online Service in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The Registration-Cum-Membership Certificate (RCMC) is a registration required by firms engaged in import and export of goods under the Foreign Trade Policy of India. These are issued by the Export Promotion Councils (EPCs) / Commodity Boards or other competent authority to a merchant exporter or manufacturer exporter in respect of a particular product or products. RCMC registration provides an enabler to get the facility of benefit under following Foreign Trade and Policy in India. Professionals Law extends full support to achieve RCMC registration and assures that the businesses are compliant by the DGFT norms by facilitating growth in export opportunities and grabbing different government schemes.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: RCMC (Registration-Cum-Membership Certificate) is very much required for exporters in order to be a registered exporters and also to meet the legal formalities with Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) authorities in order to claim benefits for duty exemptions and export incentives / benefits / rewards under different schemes of the government. It is also the requirement for joining the respective Export Promotion Council or Commodity Board. Professionals Law guarantees fast and accurate RCMC registration to get your business running seamlessly in international markets and maximize some benefit of export promotion schemes.

categories.applicantType: The following entities are eligible for RCMC registration:
criteria: Manufacturers, traders and service providers engaged in the export of goods or services.
Sole proprietorships, partnerships, LLPs, private limited companies, public limited companies, societies and trusts.
Entities must possess a valid Import Export Code (IEC) issued by DGFT.
Exporters of products under a particular Export Promotion Council or Commodity Board jurisdiction.
Professionals Law helps businesses assess their eligibility and identify the correct Export Promotion Council or Commodity Board for RCMC registration.

body.title: Access to Export Incentives
body.description: Registered exporters can claim various government export benefits under FTP, SEIS, MEIS, RoDTEP and other incentive schemes.

body.title: Enhanced Credibility
body.description: RCMC membership boosts business credibility in global markets, making foreign buyers more confident in transacting.

body.title: Support and Guidance
body.description: Export Promotion Councils offer valuable market intelligence, trade facilitation services and government support for registered members.

body.title: Participation in International Trade Fairs
body.description: RCMC holders can participate in trade fairs, buyer-seller meets and business delegations organized by EPCs and government agencies.

body.title: Smooth Customs Clearance
body.description: Customs authorities often require RCMC for processing various export clearances, duty benefits and refunds.

categories.applicantType: The general list of documents required for RCMC registration includes:
documents: Copy of Import Export Code (IEC) Certificate.
PAN Card of the business entity.
Company Incorporation Certificate / Partnership Deed / Registration Certificate.
MOA & AOA (for companies) / Trust Deed / Society Bylaws (if applicable).
Company's foreign exchange earnings by certified by Chartered Accountant (past three years)
A board resolution or power of attorney issued in favour of the signing authority
KYC Documents of authorised signatories (Email id, Mobile No. PAN, Aadhaar, Voter id, Bank Statement etc.)
Professionals Law ensures that all required documents are carefully reviewed and submitted accurately to avoid any delays or rejections.

body.description: Evaluate your export products and identify the appropriate Export Promotion Council or Commodity Board.
Gather and verify all required documents to ensure completeness.
File the online or offline RCMC application with the relevant council along with prescribed fees.
Respond to any clarifications or queries raised by the Export Promotion Council.
Once approved, the council issues the Registration-Cum-Membership Certificate.
Professionals Law provides continuous support for renewals, amendments and compliance with council guidelines.

body.title: Document Preparation
body.description: 3 to 5 working days

body.title: Application Filing
body.description: 1 to 2 working days

body.title: Processing by Export Promotion Council
body.description: 10 to 15 working days

body.title: Total Estimated Time
body.description: 2 to 3 weeks

body.title: Ineligibility for Export Benefits
body.description: Ineligibility to claim export incentives and government benefits.

body.title: Application Rejection
body.description: Rejection of export-related applications and refund claims.

body.title: Export Suspension
body.description: Suspension of export privileges under the DGFT’s Foreign Trade Policy.

body.title: Severe Non-Compliance
body.description: Penalty or suspension of licence in extreme cases of non-compliance.

body.title: Professionals Law Support
body.description: With Professionals Law, businesses can avoid these risks through timely registration and ongoing compliance support.

body.question: Is RCMC mandatory for all exporters?
body.answer: RCMC is mandatory for exporters who wish to avail benefits under the Foreign Trade Policy. In some cases, certain exports may not require RCMC if no benefit is being claimed.

body.question: Which Export Promotion Council should I register with?
body.answer: You need to register with the EPC related to your product category. Professionals Law assists in identifying the correct council.

body.question: Is separate RCMC required for each product?
body.answer: Generally, one RCMC per council suffices. If you deal in multiple products under different councils, you may need separate RCMCs.

body.question: Can service exporters apply for RCMC?
body.answer: Yes, service providers can register with the Services Export Promotion Council (SEPC) to obtain RCMC.

body.question: What is the validity of RCMC?
body.answer: RCMC is usually valid for 5 years and is renewable upon payment of renewal fees.

body.question: Can new businesses apply for RCMC?
body.answer: Yes, even newly established businesses with a valid IEC can apply for RCMC.

body.question: Is physical verification required for RCMC registration?
body.answer: Generally, physical verification is not mandatory unless specifically requested by the council.

body.question: What happens if RCMC is not obtained?
body.answer: Without RCMC, exporters may lose access to export incentives, duty exemptions and may face delays in customs clearance.

body.question: How can Professionals Law help with RCMC registration?
body.answer: Professionals Law offers complete assistance in documentation, filing, liaising with EPCs, obtaining the certificate and managing post-registration compliance.
